title_original: Quantum Tech Beats Russian Satellite Jamming in New Arms Race
summary_original: Q-CTRL, a quantum technology contractor to the United States government, announced its quantum sensors and navigation software—designed to counter sophisticated Global Positioning System jamming—will be deployed commercially later this year. CEO Michael Biercuk emphasized that quantum sensors using Earth's magnetic field and gravity can serve as resilient alternatives to satellite-based Global Positioning System, which have faced over 1,000 daily jamming incidents since late 2023, largely attributed to Russian activity over Ukraine. Q-CTRL is also working with the United States Department of Defense, Lockheed Martin, the Defense Innovation Unit, Airbus, the Royal Australian Navy, and DARPA. While quantum technology has often been hyped, these deployments mark “a priority for President Donald Trump’s White House and the Pentagon” in addressing real-world vulnerabilities.
